era: 2002-present. Seattle, Washington. Hip-Hop/Electronic production. Saba Mohajerjasbi (Iranian-American, UW graduate). Producer of Blue Scholars (degree 99) and Common Market (degree 101). Solo production: jazz-influenced, sample-based, layered. E=5. Genre Stage 98 (Hip-Hop Production enters).

location.origin: Seattle, Washington. University of Washington. Iranian-American family.
location.formative: UW Seattle -- where Sabzi met Geo (Blue Scholars). Capitol Hill and broader Seattle hip-hop scene. KEXP sessions. Mass Line Media.
location.career: Sabzi -- producer. Blue Scholars (2002-present, with Geo). Common Market (with RA Scion). Solo production under own name. Jazz sampling, layered construction, boom-bap drums.

associated_musicians.name: Blue Scholars
associated_musicians.note: Sabzi + Geo (Prometheus Brown / George Quibuyen). Seattle conscious hip-hop. The first node in the producer web.

associated_musicians.name: Common Market
associated_musicians.note: Sabzi + RA Scion (Ryan Abeo). Jazz-rap expansion. The second node.

associated_musicians.name: Smokey Brights
associated_musicians.note: Both E=5, both layered production. Synth-forward vs. sample-forward.

associated_musicians.name: Mark Lanegan
associated_musicians.note: Three consecutive dark/atmospheric entries (120-122). E=4 → E=5 → E=5.

theory_nodes.concept: The sample architecture (one hundred twenty-third acoustic element -- jazz-influenced beat construction from deconstructed samples; crate-dug source material; multiple layers; boom-bap drums; melodic hooks; warmth; analog-leaning; records as raw material)
examples: Sabzi (degree 122) -- hip-hop/electronic production. E=5. The sample architecture.
Jazz samples: piano loops, horn stabs, bass lines from the American jazz catalog.
Layered construction: multiple sample sources stacked, primary + secondary harmonic support.
Boom-bap drums: punchy kick, snapping snare, shuffling hi-hat. Classic hip-hop foundation.
Electronic expansion: synthesizers and digital processing beyond pure sampling.
theory_nodes.description: The sample architecture is the 123rd acoustic element. Jazz-influenced beat landscapes from deconstructed samples.

ancestors: Blue Scholars production (degree 99) -- the same producer, applied to conscious hip-hop with Geo.
Common Market production (degree 101) -- same producer, different emcee.
The moody pulse (degree 121) -- synthetic density. The sample architecture is organic-sourced density.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred twenty-three acoustic elements across one hundred twenty-two degrees. Track element 124 at degree 123.

theory_nodes.concept: The producer web (connection pattern -- one production figure creating connections across multiple engine entries; Sabzi → Blue Scholars 99 → Common Market 101 → Sabzi solo 122; three nodes, one figure; the producer as constant, emcees as variables; personal web spanning 23 degrees)
examples: Sabzi (degree 122): three engine degrees linked to one producer (99, 101, 122).
Blue Scholars (99): Sabzi + Geo. Common Market (101): Sabzi + RA Scion. Sabzi solo (122).
23 degrees separate first node (99) and third node (122). Tightest connection web.
In hip-hop, the producer is the constant. The production DNA is recognizable across all three entries.
theory_nodes.description: The producer web at degree 122. Three nodes, one figure. The tightest connection web in the engine.

ancestors: Blue Scholars (degree 99) -- first node. Common Market (degree 101) -- second node.
genealogy.relationship: Producer web established. Track whether additional Sabzi-produced entries appear.

theory_nodes.concept: Hip-Hop Production enters (Genre Stage 98 -- the producer examined independently of emcees; the beat as art object; production as its own creative discipline; distinct from hip-hop performance)
examples: Sabzi (degree 122): solo production under own name, independent of Blue Scholars/Common Market.
Making Arrangements (2010): instrumental album, the purest expression of production without vocals.
Genre Stage 98 recognizes that hip-hop production is its own creative discipline.
The beat carries the DNA. The producer is the architect; the emcee inhabits the architecture.
theory_nodes.description: Hip-Hop Production enters at Genre Stage 98. The beat as art object, the producer as artist.

ancestors: Hip-Hop (multiple prior degrees). The genre evolves from performance-focused to production-focused.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 98 at degree 122. Track recurrence of production-focused entries.

cross_references.relationship: PRODUCER WEB: FIRST NODE. Sabzi + Geo. Conscious hip-hop. The beat foundation. Degree 99 to 122: the producer examined alone after 23 degrees.
PRODUCER WEB: SECOND NODE. Sabzi + RA Scion. Jazz-rap expansion. Same production DNA, different emcee.
CONSECUTIVE ELECTRONIC ADJACENCY. Both E=5, both layered production. Synth-forward vs sample-forward. Different tools, shared density.
POST-ONE-THIRD TRAJECTORY. Three consecutive dark/atmospheric entries (120-122). E=4 → E=5 → E=5. Escalating density.
SEATTLE PRODUCTION DEPTH. Both E=5. Post-rock drone vs hip-hop sampling. The city produces world-class producers across genres.
